forecast.name: Tonight
forecast.detailedForecast: Partly cloudy, with a low around 11. Southwest wind 6 to 10 mph.

forecast.name: Wednesday
forecast.shortForecast: Light Snow then Chance Light Rain
forecast.detailedForecast: Snow between 7am and noon, then a chance of rain between noon and 2pm. Cloudy, with a high near 40. Southwest wind 8 to 15 mph, with gusts as high as 29 mph. Chance of precipitation is 100%. New snow accumulation of 1 to 2 inches possible.

weather_report:
The night blankets Boston in a calm darkness, punctuated by the soft twinkling of city lights, pushing the chill of the evening into the background. With temperatures poised to drop to around 11 degrees, a southwest breeze whispers through the streets, gentle yet biting, urging residents to seek refuge within their warm abodes. As the clock strikes morning, the city will awaken to a canvas of white, where delicate snowflakes will fall, tenderly blanketing the ground. The forecast promises a steady snowfall, interspersed with the possibility of rain as the day progresses, with the mercury hovering near 40 degrees. 

Tomorrow presents a blend of the frosty and the wet, inviting residents to dress in layers—perhaps a warm sweater beneath a sturdy coat, complemented by a snug scarf and sensible boots, perfect for navigating snowy sidewalks or puddle-filled streets. With gusts that could whisk away the unprepared, an umbrella might be prudent, allowing one to brave the elements while enjoying the rhythm of the snow. As the mid-morning draws near, it might be best to indulge in the simple pleasures of the season—perhaps a cozy coffee shop visit, where one can savor a rich brew while watching the snowfall from a window seat. There’s something enchanting about the world softening under fresh snow, a quiet reminder to slow down and relish the moment.
